Description: Operations return close to baseline after inferred compressor replacement. CW1 cycle durations recover to ~720s. Compressor load-sharing partially restored (correlation CW1-CW2: -0.57 vs -0.71 baseline). Some residual instability: five intermittent pressure dips with min < 0.5 still observed, suggesting the replaced compressor has not fully bedded in.

CW1 compressor fails to maintain pneumatic pressure. Catastrophic pressure dives: 0.11 on 20.02 07:15, 0.02 on 21.02 06:00. Both compressors run simultaneously up to 100% of the time, trying to compensate. CW1 short-cycling extreme: median ON-cycle drops to 45s on 20.02. Compressor replacement required.

CW1 compressor shows early degradation. Median ON-cycle duration drops from ~1440s baseline to <250s. The compressor load-sharing protocol breaks down: both CW1 and CW2 compressors begin running simultaneously, which does not happen in normal operation (baseline co-run rate: 0.85%). Pressure repeatedly drops below 0.7 during this window.

The blue line (CW1_COMPRESSOR_RUNNING) shows highly regular, periodic cycles. The orange line (CW1_MAIN_RESERVOIR_PRESSURE) consistently oscillates within a healthy, stable upper band (roughly between 0.8 and 1.0).
Both lines drop, compressors nearly stop

This marks the exact boundary where the system transitions from a healthy, predictable state to anomalous behavior. The regular, periodic duty cycle of Compressor 1 (CW1_COMPRESSOR_RUNNING) completely breaks down, flatlining near zero for an extended duration.
CW1 compressor activity drops to 16% while CW2 rises to 73% first sign of CW1 module failure
Sharp spike to 1.0 on both lines simultaneously
CW1 consistently running at 27% vs normal 35%. Never fully recovers confirmed compressor module failure
compressor starts switching on and off more frequently

The Anomaly: The compressor enters a high-frequency "short-cycling" state (visible as the dense blue block). The component is turning on and off rapidly to keep up with system demand. The Pressure Drop: During the second flagged window (Feb 22, 20:02 to Feb 23, 11:15), despite the intense compressor activity, the reservoir pressure cannot stabilize. The rapid short-cycling indicates the system is working significantly harder to maintain minimum operating pressure, pointing to a persistent system leak or major efficiency loss in the pump mechanism.
